THE COURT FINDS THAT THE AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS TO DEMONSTRATE GROSS NEGLIGENCE AND DEFENDANT RAISES A FACTUAL ISSUE THAT WOULD BE BETTER RESOLVED ON A SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION. THE DEMURRER TO THE "INTENTIONAL TORT" CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES A CLAIM FOR ACTIVE CONCEALMENT/NONDISCLOSURE. PLAINTIFF MAY BE ABLE TO ESTABLISH CONCEALMENT/NONDISCLOSURE, BUT THERE ARE INSUFFICIENT ALLEGATIONS TO SHOW INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT THE TRAINER ADVISED PLAINTIFF TO STAND ON THE FLAT SIDE OF THE BOSU "AND BY DOING SO IMPLIED AND REPRESENTED TO PLAINTIFF THAT IT WAS SAFE TO DO SO." DEFENDANT'S ONLY STATEMENT WAS FOR PLAINTIFF TO STAND ON THE BOSU. THERE WAS NO EXPRESS REPRESENTATION THAT IT WAS SAFE TO DO SO. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication By Defendant 24 Hour Fitness Usa Inc.
Off Calendar Jun-09-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nelson Barry and Agatha Panday of Law Office of Nelson C. Barry telephone number 415-587-0550 for Plaintiff Lamis Malouf.
Derek Classen of Prindle, Goetz, Barnes & Reinholtz LLP telephone number 415-788-8354 for Defendant 24 Hour Fitness.
Stephen Jenkins of Mannion, Gaynor & Manning LLP telephone number 415-512-4381 for Defendant BOSU Fitness, LLC.
Steven Myers of Law Offices of John A. Biard telephone number 925-945-4435 appeared by telephone for Cross-Defendant Ball Bounce and Sport, Inc.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court modified its tentative ruling. The Court is not granting summary adjudication as to issue 4 (concealment cause of action) and will strike it from the order. DEFENDANT 24 HOUR FITNESS USA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is granted and denied in part. The court denies summary adjudication as to issues 1-3 (application of primary assumption of the risk, release, and 10th defense (release/waiver)). There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the primary assumption of the risk doctrine bars plaintiff's claims. Specifically, there is a disputed issue of fact whether Ms. Carey "acted recklessly in the sense that the instructor's conduct was 'totally outside the range of the ordinary activity.'" Kahn v. East Side Union High School (2003) 31 Cal.4th 990, 1011. The evidence shows that: (1) BOSU warned that standing on the platform side of the ball was "not recommended" (Ex. D to Barry Dec.); (2) plaintiff had only used the BOSU ball 3 times (Malouf Depo. 42:3-7); (3) despite plaintiff's lack of experience/training (unfitness to perform the activity), Ms. Carey had plaintiff stand on the platform side of the BOSU; (4) Ms. Carey left plaintiff and failed to "spot" her (Malouf Depo., 43:4-44:8); and (5) the trier of fact could find that Ms. Carey acted recklessly (Buck Depo., 68:20-69:12). "A release cannot absolve a party from liability for gross negligence." Jimenez v. 24 Hour Fitness USA, Inc. (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 546, 554. The above evidence creates a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the release bars plaintiff's recovery. In other words, there is a dispute whether defendant's conduct constituted an extreme departure from the ordinary standard of conduct such that defendant committed gross negligence. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/SB)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-22-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nelson Barry III of Law Office of Nelson C. Barry telephone number 415-587-0550 for Plaintiff.
Derek Classen of Prindle, Amaro, Goetz, Hillyard, Barnes & Reinholtz telephone number 562-436-3946 for Defendant 24 Hour Fitness.

Mr. Barry advised the Court that he withdraws his opposition and accepts the tentative ruling in its entirety. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT 24 HOUR FITNESS USA, INC'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - The demurrer to the gross negligence cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to plead as part of the negligence cause of action. The court finds that the amended complaint alleges sufficient facts to demonstrate gross negligence and defendant raises a factual issue that would be better resolved on a summary judgment motion. The demurrer to the "intentional tort" cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges a claim for active concealment/nondisclosure. The demurrer to the fraud (intentional misrepresentation) cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff may be able to establish concealment/nondisclosure, but there are insufficient allegations to show intentional misrepresentation. Plaintiff alleges that the trainer advised plaintiff to stand on the flat side of the BOSU "and by doing so implied and represented to plaintiff that it was safe to do so." Defendant's only statement was for plaintiff to stand on the BOSU. There was no express representation that it was safe to do so. Order signed in open Court.
